observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and the management agreed to:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to timely submit the SSMI together with the RIS, ICS and other documents to the Municipal Accountant to record the issuances in the books;

Direct the Municipal Accountant to strengthen monitoring procedures on outstanding inventories and regularly communicate with the Municipal Treasurer to submit SSMI supported with proper documents to support recording of issuances in the books; and

Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of inventories semestral and submit the RPCI to the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer for reconciliation in their records copy furnish COA.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough review, with the involvement of other concerned personnel, and analyze the details of receivables to ascertain their correctness, validity and collectability and send demand letters to persons who have known addresses to facilitate collections; and

File a request for a write-off to the COA the account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected which should be duly supported with certified relevant documents pertaining to the accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Committee on Family, Women, Children, and Elderly of the SB endorse the passing of an ordinance localizing RA No. 11313 and post the same in conspicuous places. Further, we recommend that the ordinance include provisions on the following: a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof; and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to facilitate regular reconciliation of cashbook balances and the books of account of the LGU at least quarterly in compliance with the aforementioned regulation.

We recommended for Management to direct the:
Municipal GSO/Treasurer to cause the immediate preparation of the Inventory and IIRUP and to submit the same to COA for inspection and/or appraisal.

Municipal Appraisal Committee, created for the purpose, to conduct initial valuation according to property classification and condition and to set the floor price to expedite the determination of price quoted from interested buyer based on the results of the auction sale or negotiation to be conducted, as may warrant, following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 92-386, to prevent further deterioration of the properties and to obtain prices most advantages to the LGU, if found to be saleable; and

Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments in the books once disposal records have been prepared and verified

We recommended that management:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to timely and regularly prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Fuel Consumption Report for monitoring and controlling purposes, and to submit these reports to the Auditor for verification and evaluation of the reasonableness of fuel consumption in a specific month; 

Direct the Municipal the Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments and comply strictly with the provisions of Sections 114 of the Manual on NGAS for the proper recording of purchased fuel, oil and lubricants; and 

Direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to coordinate and exert extra effort to prepare and submit valid documents for liquidation of the year-end balances that are still carried in the books as of December 31, 2021.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The Municipal Treasurer/GSO to consolidate the RIS, prepare the SSMI, and submit it to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books of accounts.
The Municipal Accountant to record the issuances in the books of accounts, upon receipt of the SSMI together with RIS and other documents from the Municipal Treasurer.
Install monitoring mechanisms in each of the Offices on the issuances of inventories such that all issuances of inventories are accounted for and documented.
The Inventory Committee to ensure that physical count of inventories is conducted semestrally and submit the RPCI to the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer for reconciliation in their records, copy furnish the COA.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the management agreed to the following:
The HRMO, during budget preparations of the Annual Budget for the year, to include not only the retirable/co-terminus employees and elected officials during the Annual Budget period but also those employees/officials who are to retire and whose term of service will end in the ensuing year in the computation of the TLB;
The Local Finance Committee to prioritize the allocation of leave benefits computed by the HRMO in the Annual Budget preparation, and
The Municipal Accountant, to:
Recognize in the books of accounts the unrecorded leave benefits payable and subsequently, the leave benefits which are expected to be settled within 12 months after the end of the reporting period. The proposed adjusting entries are as follows:
Prior Period Adjustments			P5,296,481.33
Leave Benefits Payable				P5,296,481.33
To recognize prior years’ accumulated earned leave credits
as of December 31, 2024.
Suggested accounting entries upon payment:
Leave Benefits Payable
Terminal Leave Benefits*
Prior Period Adjustments**
Cash in Bank
To record payment of accumulated earned leave credits
*Leave credits earned during CY 2025
** PYs’ leave benefits due to increase of salary in CY 2025; and
Ensure to record transactions with the correct account name and code in the books of accounts.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer request the disposal of unserviceable properties by filling up the IIRUP and proceed with disposal procedures in accordance with COA DBM Joint Circular No. 2024-01 and submit to the Municipal Accountant the duly approved IIRUP and disposal documents for recording in the books of accounts, after the completion of the disposal process.
Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant agreed to recognize impairment loss by P338,800.00 on the unserviceable garbage compactor from the books of accounts.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the management agreed that:
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to secure copies of documents and other pertinent records of the donated assets from the donor in order to support the recording in the books of account; and
To create an Appraisal Committee to appraise the fair market value of the donated PPEs in case documents for its cost measurement are not successfully obtained from the source agencies.

Recommendations
We recommended, and management agreed that:
The Municipal Treasurer/GSO to prepare the List of PPEs Found at the Station and the List of PPEs Non-Existing/Missing PPEs based on the RPCPPE prepared by the Inventory Committee, and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books following the procedures in COA Circular on derecognition, if necessary; and
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal TreasurerGSO to:
Reconcile their records with the updated RPCPPE of the Inventory Committee; and
Prepare and update their property and ledger cards.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management through the Municipal Accountant, agreed to properly disclose in the Notes to FS the following:
Aggregate remuneration of key management and those who are already retired for the year and those who are retiring in the next reporting period, as well as the Municipality’s adoption of IPSAS 39 in accordance with COA Circular No. 2024-009; and provide disclosure in the Notes to the FS on the nature of the leave benefits payable and its expected payment for at least the following year;
Reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities to Surplus/(Deficit) in the Notes to the FS as provided for in the aforementioned regulations; and
Accurate reported balances of LDRRMF in the Notes to FS.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the LSB agreed to ensure that:
A copy of the approved SEF Budget is furnished to the Municipal Budget Officer, Accountant, and Treasurer not later than seven days after the approval of the SEF Budget; and
SEF utilization reports are submitted quarterly not later than the 20th day after the end of the quarter and annually not later than the 15th day of February of the following year, to the DepEd CO, through appropriate channels, and copy furnish the local sanggunian concerned as well as the regional offices of the DBM and DILG.

Recommendation
Moving forward, we recommended, and Management agreed to closely supervise and monitor project progress and implement remedial measures and interventions to ensure timely completion of PRDPs.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the GFPS agreed to:
Include in the Annual GPB the attendance to seminars on  GeRL tool to capacitate themself in the conduct of gender audit; and
Conduct a gender audit on its agency through the use of the GeRL tool.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed that the LDRRM Officer to:
Prepare Budgetary Program for their counterpart for the procurement of a fire truck charged to  the STF for approval of the Municipal Mayor, and adoption of  the LDRRM Council; and
Include in their CY 2025 LDRRMFIP the approved Budgetary program for the procurement of a fire truck charged to the STF.

Recommendation
We recommended, and LDRRM Officer agreed to prepare the LDRRMFIP for the current year appropriation for LDRRMF, and incorporate therein the PPAs charged to the unexpended LDRRMF in previous years or STF duly supported by the approved Budgetary Program for that purpose.

Recommendation
We recommended, and LDRRM Officer agreed to submit the monthly Report on  Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to COA on or before the 15th day after the end of  each month.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to fully institutionalize the LDRRM Office by filling up the mandatory positions to perform its duties and responsibilities including administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning as well as the execution of LDRRMF PPAs.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure complete  documentation of every transaction and adhere to the rules and regulations as provided above before payment is made to the payee in consonance with pertinent rules and regulations.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to refrain from granting any additional allowances,  incentives, or other financial benefits to officials and employees unless expressly authorized by law.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to issue a memorandum order enjoining all officers and employees with unliquidated CAs to immediately liquidate the same with a provision instructing the Municipal Treasurer to hold their claims/salaries unless  their CAs are fully liquidated.

Recommendation
We recommended, and BAC agreed to invite the observers in writing and send/notify them within five calendar days before each procurement activity.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the BAC to strictly follow the procurement procedures prescribed in RA No. 9184 and its RIRR and exercise due diligence in ensuring that the award of contract shall only be made to suppliers with complete eligibility documents submitted during the opening of quotations.

Recommendations
We recommended and the BAC agreed to:
Evaluate each procurement request to determine the appropriate  procurement mode to ensure that the correct mode of procurement is  conducted;
Refrain from utilizing the Shopping method for procurement transactions if  they do not meet the conditions specified in cases a and b of Section 52 in  the RIRR of RA No. 9184; and
Consider the application of Negotiated procurement through the SVP method under Section 53.9 of RIRR, RA 9184.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and identify the propriety of credit and debit memoranda and thereafter draw a JEV to record and adjust the reconciling items in the books and ensure that reconciling items are timely adjusted to generate accurate financial reporting as of a given period.

We recommended, and the management agreed to direct the:
Municipal Accountant to analyze the unreconciled balances of the Due from and Due to Other Funds account, identify the reconciling items and prepare the necessary adjusting JEV to reflect the correct account balances in the Statement of Financial Position as of year-end and strictly monitor the reciprocal accounts by conducting regular reconciliation thereof, to eliminate and avoid future discrepancies, if any; and

Municipal Treasurer to promptly deposit checks due to other funds in the Authorized Government Depository Banks of the LGU to facilitate the correct balance of reciprocal accounts in the Financial Statements.
We recommended, and the management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal treasurer to immediately return the excess funds to the SA.

We recommended, and the management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to require the business owners to submit a sworn statement of gross sales/receipts/Audited Financial Statements if applicable, and assessment of business taxes should be based thereon and conduct examination on books of accounts in cases of discovered under declaration of gross sales or receipts by taxpayers to establish effective enforcement and collection of taxes, fees and charges as provided in the Ordinance.

Moving forward, we recommended and the management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer on the unobligated balance of LDRRMF and prepare DV to effect the transfer the unexpended current balance of the LDDRMF in the GF to TF as at year-end and not merely as set-up of obligation, and the Municipal Treasurer to draw a check and deposit the same to the TF account to effect the transfer.

We recommended, and the Management agreed that the GAD GFPS formulate a GAD Agenda for the remaining term of the LCE in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and be made as a basis in the preparation of the annual GPB.

We recommended that Management to:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the preparation of the duly certified list showing the names of all taxpayers and the tax due from each taxpayer and furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year as a basis of the latter for the booking up of the RPT and SET Receivable accounts;

Direct the Municipal Accountant to make a detailed analysis of the Receivable accounts and their contra accounts to determine the cause/s of the discrepancies and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts; and

Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant undertake periodic reconciliation of the balances per master list of RPT delinquencies with that of the RPT and SET Receivable account balances per books of accounts to come up with a more accurate and proper balance of RPT and SET Receivable accounts.

To comply with Administrative Order (AO) No. 7., s. 2003 dated April 14, 2003 for the creation of IAS and the implementing guidelines under DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-4 dated March 22, 2004 for the organization and staffing thereof.

We recommended that Management:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct thorough review, with the involvement of other concerned personnel, and analyze the details of receivables to ascertain their correctness, validity and collectability and send demand letters to persons who have known address to facilitate collections; and

File a request for write-off to the Commission on Audit the account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected which should be duly supported with certified relevant documents pertaining to the accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to:

Regularly monitor the movement of the cash advances of concerned AOs in order to restrict granting of additional cash advances to those with existing balances, as well as to better oversee the timely liquidation of the same; and

Continue issuing demand letters requiring the liquidation of cash advances to the AOs concerned.

We recommended for Management to direct the:
Municipal GSO/Treasurer to cause the immediate preparation of the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and to submit the same to COA for inspection and/or appraisal.

Municipal Appraisal Committee, created for the purpose, to conduct initial valuation according to property classification and condition and to set the floor price to expedite the determination of price quoted from interested buyer based on the results of the auction sale or negotiation to be conducted, as may warrant, following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 92-386, to prevent further deterioration of the properties and to obtain prices most advantages to the LGU, if found to be saleable; and

Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments in the books once disposal records have been prepared and verified.

We recommended that management:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to timely and regularly prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Fuel Consumption Report for monitoring and controlling purposes, and to submit these reports to the Auditor for verification and evaluation of the reasonableness of fuel consumption in a specific month; 

Direct the Municipal the Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments and comply strictly with the provisions of Sections 114 of the Manual on NGAS for the proper recording of purchased fuel, oil and lubricants; and 

Direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to coordinate and exert extra effort to prepare and submit valid documents for liquidation of the year end balances that are still carried in the books as of December 31, 2021.

To comply with AO No. 7., s. 2003 dated April 14, 2003 for the creation of IAS and the implementing guidelines under DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-4 dated March 22, 2004 for the organization and staffing thereof.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the:
Inventory Committee to:
Conduct the one-time physical inventory of PPEs under the name of the LGU and relieve each member of their regular functions to devote themselves in completing the same;
Prepare the PIP, and submit the same to the Municipal Mayor for approval to ensure  the proper and adequate conduct of inventory-taking pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006;
Submit a copy of the approved PIP to COA at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities;
Use pre-numbered property stickers bearing the vital information on the PPE items, such as
Description of the Property
Model Number
Serial Number
Acquisition Date / Cost
Person Accountable
Space for the Validation / Signature of the Inventory Committee
in the conduct of physical inventory and attach the same to the prominent and secured portion of the inventoried PPE for proper and easy identification;
Prepare a consolidated RPCPPE to be used for  reconciliation of PPE items recorded in the GSO and Accounting Office.;
Strictly implement COA Circular No. 2020-006 in the conduct of inventory of PPE.
Municipal Treasurer as the Designated Municipal General Service Officer (GSO) to:
Use pre-numbered property tags or control stickers to all PPE containing the information required under Section 5.7 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and attach the same to the prominent and secured portion of the inventoried PPE for proper and easy identification;
Ensure that all issuances of PPE items are covered with PARs as prescribed under Section 5.6 of COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
Renew/Update issued PARs every three (3) years after issuance or whenever there is a change in custodianship to establish proper accountability.
Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and Inventory Committee to coordinate and complete the physical inventory-taking and conduct the reconciliation of the RPCPPE; identify and address the nature and causes of variances; and hold responsible/accountable the designated officers/custodians.
Municipal Accountant to record adjustments after completion of proper reconciliation to establish the correct balances of PPE and other affected accounts, with the presence of valid supporting documents

Recommendations
We recommended, and the management agreed to require:
The Municipal Accountant to:
Apply the criteria on capitalization of PPE as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2022-004 to ensure proper classification of assets in the books;
Draw a Journal Entry Voucher  to effect necessary adjustments in the books of accounts in CY 2024 as follows:
Debit: Accumulated Depreciation	       P6,144,896.96
Prior Period Adjustment                 P3,734,488.68
Credit:      Property, Plant and Equipment              P9,879,385.64
The Municipal Treasurer to:
Provide data to the Municipal Accountant relevant to items reclassified as semi-expendable property including information of its condition;
Update existing PPE and Inventory records and prepare and maintain the additional necessary forms/reports/registries for purposes of record-keeping, and monitoring  over issued semi-expendable properties provided in Section 4 of the Circular;
Cancel the Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) of the semi-expendable property and issue Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) to the actual custodian of the items; and
Moving forward, issue ICS to actual custodians of items toto establish monitoring, control and accountability over them.

Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of inventories semestrally and submit the RPCI to the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer for reconciliation in their records copy furnish COA.

Continue issuing demand letters requiring the liquidation of cash advances to the AOs concerned.

Recommendation
We recommended, and the Mmnagement agreed that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) formulate a GAD Agenda for the remaining term of the LCE in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and be made as basis in the preparation of the annual GPB.

Recommendation
We recommended and the Mangement agreed that the Committee on Family, Women, Children, and Elderly of the SB endorse the passing of an ordinance localizing RA No. 11313 and post the same in conspicuous places. Further, we recommend that the ordinance include provisions on the following: a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof; and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to exert efforts to retrieve records for the reconstruction of depreciation schedule reports on all depreciable assets to facilitate fair presentation of the PPE and depreciation expense balances at all times.

Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to undertake a comprehensive review on the depreciation schedule and make the required adjustments in the books once the updating of the assets’ depreciable amount and the corresponding accumulated depreciation have been verified.
We recommended the Management to direct the following:

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to check the said items and be specific in recording transaction in the CIP account in order to properly monitor the transfer of the items to appropriate asset account.

We recommended to the LCE to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to gather related documents pertaining to recorded CIP. On the basis thereof, prepare necessary adjusting entries for the recognition of corresponding expense account and reflect the correct amount of the CIP; and

Require the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to review and evaluate project cost and status of implementation of all the recorded projects under the CIP accounts to facilitate recording/transfer of its cost to the appropriate account classification.

We recommended to the LCE:

To direct the Municipal Budget Officer to submit the SAAOB for the year ending December 31, 2022 to the office of the Municipal Accountant and Office of the COA Team Leader; and 

To direct the Municipal Accountant to complete the data reflected in the SCBAA after the submission of SAAOB.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

Update the Cash in Local Treasury and Cash in Bank cashbooks by recording all collections, deposits and disbursements daily in order to reflect/establish the accountability at any given date to facilitate early detection of errors and the subsequent correction thereof; and

Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to facilitate regular reconciliation of cashbook balances and the books of account of the LGU at least quarterly in compliance with the aforementioned regulation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

Send written notices to the payees to verify the existence of the stale checks. Replacement check may be issued upon submission of the stale check;

Practice the sending of written notices to the payees at least one (1) month  before a check becomes stale; and

Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the necessary adjustment/s in the books of accounts, if any.

We recommended the Management to assign a personnel to take charge of the submission to the Auditor of copies of duly perfected contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents within five (5) working days from perfection thereof for prompt review and evaluation.

Moreover, to properly monitor such compliance thereof within the reglementary period as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 so that any deficiency noted in the review thereof could be immediately complied/rectified by management.

We recommended that Management direct the concerned personnel to fast track the implementation of the unimplemented or delayed projects/programs under the CY 2022 20% Development Fund current and continuing appropriations.

Further, it is also recommended that Management provide specific timeline for the implementation of the planned projects/programs in the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) and APP to ensure

The management to:

Direct the personnel concerned in providing the necessary data on the transactions presented as to the basic information of the description of the items and the quantity of goods received by the beneficiaries;

Designate a Property Officer/Custodian who will facilitate reporting and monitoring of all inventoriable goods procured and distributed; and

Monitor the Property Officer/Custodian on the preparation and maintenance of necessary reports and inventory list of all procured and donated items in accordance with COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.

The Municipality enhance its compliance with RA 9003 by coordinating efforts of the concerned personnel as to integration of relevant costs for the implementation of the SWMP for consideration in the Annual Budget as well as Annual Investment Plan for the following year. Moreover, to properly monitor the implementation of various projects, programs and activities under Solid Waste Management Program.

The management, through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office:

The management prioritize the establishment of a permanent evacuation center intended to be fully disaster-ready at all times in accordance with the set-forth standards under DILG Memorandum Circular 122 series of 2018.

To comply with AO No. 7., s. 2003 dated April 14, 2003 for the creation of Internal Audit Service (IAS) and the implementing guidelines under DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-4 dated March 22, 2004 for the organization and staffing thereof.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Management to direct the following:
The Municipal Accountant to locate necessary documents supporting the dormant items composing the CIP account; to communicate to the MEO requesting for documents such as Acceptance and Inspection Report, Report on Completed Projects and Certificate of Project Completion for these will be the basis in reclassifying or transferring of CIP to its appropriate asset account. Lastly, to record necessary adjustments in the books of accounts based on the result of verification to reflect the correct balance of the account.
The Municipal Engineering Office submit and coordinate timely with MAO on the status of the on-going and completed projects and its supporting documents to properly classify and present an accurate account balance of the CIP.
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to be specific in recording transaction in the CIP account in order to properly monitor the transfer of the items to appropriate asset account.
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to carefully verify the documents as basis in recording the transaction in the CIP account. Such shall be based on the Program of Work (POW), per project.
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to gather related documents pertaining to recorded CIP. On the basis thereof, prepare necessary adjusting entries for the recognition corresponding expense account and reflect the correct amount of the CIP.
To require the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to review and evaluate the project cost and implementation of all projects that are still recorded under the CIP accounts to facilitate recording/transfer of its cost to the appropriate account classification.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive:
To direct the Municipal Budget Officer to submit the SAAOB for the year ending of CY 2021 to the office of the Municipal Accountant and Office of the COA Team Leader; and
To direct the Municipal Accountant to complete the data reflected in the SCBAA after the submission of SAAOB.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the non-current portion of Loans Payable-Domestic indicating the current and non-current portion of the said liabilities and disclose the significant information of the account to present fairly the Financial Statement.

Recommendations:
We recommended for Management to direct the:
Municipal GSO/Treasurer to cause the immediate preparation of the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and to submit the same to COA for inspection and/or appraisal.
Municipal Appraisal Committee, created for the purpose, to conduct initial valuation according to property classification and condition and to set the floor price to expedite the determination of price quoted from interested buyer based on the results of the auction sale or negotiation to be conducted, as may warrant, following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 92-386, to prevent further deterioration of the properties and to obtain prices most advantages to the LGU, if found to be saleable; and
Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments in the books once disposal records have been prepared and verified.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare demand letters for the liquidation of the said cash advances from accountable officers and monitor the timely submission of liquidation report within the reglementary in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002;

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to desist from designating non-permanent personnel as Special Disbursing Officer pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 to avoid exposing government funds to risk of loss as well as to save the cost of bond premiums. Instead utilize existing bonded employees from the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to handle cash advances for special field activities.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management instruct the AO to stop from transferring funds to another AO to be able to closely monitor its accountability and the corresponding liquidation are expended within the purpose for which it was granted.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Government enhance its compliance with policies and procedures on Local Roads Asset Management System by:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to establish and properly maintain the Local Road Network Property Card for all Road networks and its components; using the RCPPE reports as basis;
Monitor proper reconciliation between Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and Local Road Network Ledger Card and/ Property Card and make the necessary adjustments; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant the proper disclosure in the Financial Statement of Road Network System.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to:

Post every collection and deposit in a chronological order and update her cash book on a daily basis;

In order to reduce the above cash advances, we reiterate our recommendations for management not to grant additional cash advances to officers and employees with outstanding cash advances and require them to execute written authorization to deduct from their salaries or other emoluments their outstanding cash advances in such reasonable amounts as necessary to fully settle their obligation.

observations.recommendation:

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Inventory Committee  to prioritize the completion of the physical inventory of all the LGU’s assets, prepare RPCPPE for each type of property, plant and equipment, reconcile the result thereof with the records of the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, and submit to COA for review and verification; and
Immediately investigate, clear and reconcile all discrepancies between physical account and accounting and property records.  If necessary, written explanations shall be required from accountable and responsible persons.

We recommended that:
Require the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the reconciliation of the difference between the Due to and Due from Other Funds accounts and take-up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to bring the two accounts in agreement;
Require the Municipal Accountant to direct the concerned personnel in charge of the different funds to simultaneously prepare Journal Entry Vouchers when Due to and Due from Other Funds accounts are involved so that errors can be immediately detected and corrected; and
Perform regular review and evaluation of transactions to detect errors and omissions.

We recommended that:
The Accounting Department, in collaboration with the Treasury Department, perform immediate identification and thorough analysis of the recorded trust liabilities and reconcile with the available cash balances in the Trust Fund books; and
The Municipal Accountant should also perform periodic reconciliation of the trust receipts/transfers coming into the possession of the local treasurer with due regard on the proper utilization and charges against the trust fund for control and monitoring purposes.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Treasurer execute all contracts of lease for all existing occupants and submit to us copies within thirty (30) days from receipt hereof;
Concerned LGU officials exert extra efforts to intensify collection of delinquent accounts and adopt all necessary measures to enable the collections of unpaid stall rentals and formulate plans and adopts policies and strategies to address the low occupancy of stalls in the public market with the end view of generating more income for the LGU; and
Management strictly impose all provisions of its municipal revenue code.
We recommended that management immediately submit the remaining financial reports for CY 2017.

We recommended that management:
On the Unutilized Balance of the LDRRMF:
Direct the LDRRM Council to implement the LDRRM plan according to the LDRRM Investment plan approved for the year.
Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to come up with disaster risk management activities for the LGU in order to utilize the accumulated balance of LDRRM fund in accordance to DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73 which enjoined the Local Chief Executives to utilize their DRRM Fund to ensure that basic “rescue and response equipment” are procured and to operationalize the provisions of the National DRRM Plan and National Climate Change Action Plan;
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the LDRRMC to strictly observe the distribution of the LDRRMF allocation as to the 30% Quick Response Fund and 70% for mitigation and disaster preparedness;
On Recording of Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment:
Facilitate the transfer/turn-over of the equipment to the new MDRRMO-designate and issue corresponding Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE).

We recommended that the LGU to:
Comply the requirements and make a representation or follow-up for the approval of the SWM Plan of the LGU;
Strictly observe proper charging of expenses against the 20% Development Fund and Solid Waste Management Program; and
Set up a special account the collection of fines, fees and other receipts arising from possible income of recyclable materials.

We recommended:
That the Accountant should check and verify the completeness of supporting documents attached to the vouchers/payrolls; and
That the Personnel Officer conducts a thorough review and inventory of all job orders and provide specific duties and responsibilities of job to ensure that those hired will not be performing task already being performed by the regular employees pursuant to CSC Memorandum Circular No. 1 series of 2007.
